title: Human Rights Lawyer
description: Human Rights Lawyers advocate for the protection and promotion of human rights, representing individuals and groups who have suffered abuses. They work to uphold constitutional rights, international laws, and fundamental freedoms.
content: Human Rights Lawyers in India work on a variety of issues, including caste discrimination, gender inequality, religious freedom, environmental justice, and police brutality. They may work for NGOs, international organizations, or in private practice, often handling pro bono cases or cases with significant social impact. Strong research, advocacy, and communication skills are essential.

financialFeasibility.note: While a law degree is accessible to many, pursuing specialized human rights training and working in this field often involves lower initial salaries, making it more feasible for those with some financial support. Pro bono work is common.

sectorScore.remarks: Opportunities exist in government commissions, NGOs, and private law firms specializing in human rights. International organizations also offer possibilities.

relatedCareers.reason: Shares a focus on social justice and advocating for vulnerable populations.
relatedCareers.title: Social Worker

relatedCareers.reason: Analyzes and develops policies related to human rights issues.
relatedCareers.title: Policy Analyst

relatedCareers.reason: Investigates and reports on human rights violations.
relatedCareers.title: Journalist

genderDistribution.note: Becoming increasingly balanced, with a strong presence of female lawyers in the field.
